mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in PAYEMS raises household income expectations and analyst earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if high PAYEMS 3-month momentum periods do not have higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than low momentum periods, or if walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 for three consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above its 6-month moving average by more than 0.2 percentage points, recession risk and credit stress rise, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above its 6-month moving average by >0.2pp
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases or if average triggered returns are not below unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rising FEDFUNDS trend combined with weakening RSAFS signals policy tightening into soft consumption demand, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint tightening-and-demand-slowdown state has nonnegative mean 20-day returns or if the interaction term is not negative in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and INDPRO both cross above their 6-month moving averages, broad demand and production confirm an earnings upswing, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and INDPRO both cross above their 6-month moving averages
failure_condition: Reject if jointly confirmed RSAFS and INDPRO upturns fail to outperform unconditional 60-day returns by at least 1 percentage point on average in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses below its 6-month moving average by more than 0.10 percentage points, easier policy lowers discount rates and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses below its 6-month moving average by >0.10pp
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS downward-crossing events have lower average 20-day S&P 500 returns than non-event periods or if hit rate is below 50% over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO crosses below its 3-month moving average by more than 0.5%, investors mark down near-term revenue and margin expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its 3-month moving average by >0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO downside shocks are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or if event-study mean return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST crosses above its 12-month moving average by more than 10%, construction activity and durable-goods demand accelerate, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above its 12-month moving average by >10%
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST upside-crossing events do not generate higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than all other months, or if the positive coefficient is unstable across two consecutive expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained CPIAUCSL disinflation combined with FEDFUNDS below 1% raises real income and keeps discount rates low, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month inflation crosses below its 12-month inflation rate and FEDFUNDS < 1%
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot shows CPIAUCSL at 217.224 and FEDFUNDS at 0.18
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-rate disinflation state has lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than the unconditional sample or if the CPIAUCSL-by-FEDFUNDS interaction is not positive.
